content: Stable CAD management: lifestyle modifications (smoking cessation, Mediterranean diet, exercise, weight management) plus optimal medical therapy. Antianginal medications: beta-blockers (first-line), calcium channel blockers, long-acting nitrates, ranolazine (late sodium current inhibitor). Secondary prevention: high-intensity statin (atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg), antiplatelet therapy (aspirin 75-100mg daily; clopidogrel if aspirin intolerant), ACEi/ARB (especially with HTN, DM, CKD, HFrEF), beta-blocker post-MI or with HFrEF. Revascularization indications: symptom control despite OMT, left main disease, multivessel CAD with reduced EF, large area of ischemia. PCI preferred for single-vessel or focal disease; DES (drug-eluting stents) standard. CABG preferred for left main disease, multivessel CAD with diabetes (FREEDOM trial), complex multivessel disease (higher SYNTAX score), or concomitant valve surgery. CABG provides better long-term patency and fewer repeat revascularizations but higher perioperative risk. DAPT duration after PCI: 6-12 months depending on bleeding/ischemic risk.
